case: Served 12 of 15 years for armed robbery. Completed anger management, vocational training in plumbing, and has a confirmed job offer from a licensed contractor.
reason: Rehabilitation demonstrated, skills acquired, and a job waiting.

case: Served 4 of 20 years for murder. Multiple disciplinary infractions, caught with contraband last month, refuses to participate in programmes.
reason: No rehabilitation; ongoing disciplinary issues and no plan.

case: Served 8 of 10 years for drug possession. Clean conduct record, completed rehab, family support system, and transitional housing arranged.
reason: Non-violent offence, clean record, and solid release plan.

case: Served 3 of 25 years for serial armed robbery. Three prior parole violations, known gang affiliation, and no release plan.
reason: Repeat parole violations and gang ties; high risk of reoffending.

case: Served 6 of 8 years for fraud. Completed financial literacy courses, made restitution from prison earnings, and has family housing lined up.
reason: Non-violent, restitution made, and a credible plan.

case: Served 2 of 15 years for attempted murder. Threatened a guard last week, and the victim's family reports ongoing intimidation from associates.
reason: Recent violence and ongoing intimidation; danger is clear.

case: Served 10 of 12 years for burglary. Earned a high school diploma and a welding certificate, disciplinary-free for 5 years, and has a church sponsor.
reason: Long clean record, education, and community sponsorship.

case: Served 5 of 10 years for assault. Filed no parole plan, refused all counselling, and the parole board report cites unaddressed substance abuse.
reason: No plan and unaddressed addiction; risk of relapse is high.

case: Served 14 of 18 years for drug trafficking. Became a mentor to younger inmates, earned a business degree, and has a confirmed job at a former employer.
reason: Leadership in rehabilitation, education, and employment ready.

case: Served 4 of 30 years for kidnapping. The inmate has connections to an organised crime ring and a coded letter suggesting a planned escape was intercepted.
reason: Escape plot and organised crime ties; release would endanger the public.

case: Served 9 of 10 years for theft. Completed restorative justice sessions with the victim, who supports release, and has secured employment.
reason: Victim support and employment; rehabilitation is evident.

case: Served 6 of 12 years for domestic violence. Refused to complete the batterer's intervention programme and has written threatening letters from his cell.
reason: Refused treatment and ongoing threats; risk to the victim is high.

case: Served 7 of 10 years for forgery. model conduct, earned a paralegal certificate, and a law firm has offered an administrative position.
reason: Exemplary conduct, relevant skills, and a job offer.

case: Served 3 of 20 years for armed robbery. Found with a mobile phone and drugs in his cell two weeks ago, and his social media shows gang photos posted by associates.
reason: Recent contraband and ongoing gang activity; not reformed.

case: Served 11 of 15 years for manslaughter. Genuine remorse expressed, completed grief counselling with the victim's family's blessing, and has housing with a sibling.
reason: Remorse, counselling, and family support; low risk of reoffending.

case: Served 5 of 8 years for repeat DUI. Licences still suspended, refused alcohol treatment, and states he 'doesn't have a problem.'
reason: No insight into the problem and refused treatment; risk is high.

case: Served 8 of 10 years for receiving stolen goods. Learned carpentry, saved prison wages for a fresh start, and a faith community has arranged housing and a job.
reason: Skills, savings, and structured community support.

case: Served 2 of 18 years for sexual assault. Denies the offence despite DNA evidence, refused treatment programmes, and the psychologist rates risk as high.
reason: Denial despite evidence, no treatment, and high risk assessment.

case: Served 13 of 15 years for robbery. Writes a blog about prison reform from inside, has a publisher interested in his story, and a halfway house is ready.
reason: Constructive use of time, external support, and a transition plan.

case: Served 6 of 25 years for human trafficking. Associates are still active, the proceeds were never recovered, and intelligence links him to ongoing operations.
reason: Ongoing criminal enterprise and unrecovered proceeds; risk is severe.

case: Served 9 of 12 years for burglary. His elderly mother is his only housing option and she is bedridden; he has no job and no programme completed.
reason: No viable plan or support; likely to reoffend to survive.

case: Served 10 of 14 years for drug distribution. Completed a teaching assistant qualification, mentors at-risk youth inside, and a school has offered him a janitorial role with progression.
reason: Education, mentoring, and a credible employment path.

case: Served 3 of 10 years for arson. The fire marshal's report confirmed it was reckless, not malicious. He has completed fire safety training and has a job in a warehouse.
reason: Non-malicious offence, relevant training, and employment lined up.

case: Served 4 of 16 years for aggravated assault. He has a history of violent outbursts in prison, spent time in solitary, and the psychologist notes unmanaged anger.
reason: Unmanaged anger and prison violence; risk to the public is high.

case: Served 12 of 20 years for fraud. He has repaid all victims from prison earnings, earned an accounting degree, and a former judge has written a character reference.
reason: Full restitution, education, and community backing.

case: Served 5 of 12 years for robbery. Three associates from the original crime are still at large and were seen visiting; no parole plan has been filed.
reason: Contact with fugitive associates and no plan; risk of reoffending.

case: Served 8 of 10 years for petty theft. He is 72 years old, in declining health, and his daughter is a nurse who can care for him; the offence was non-violent.
reason: Elderly, non-violent, and has a carer; no public safety risk.

case: Served 3 of 15 years for burglary. He attempted to escape six months ago and was found with a map of the prison perimeter and a smuggled phone.
reason: Recent escape attempt; clearly not ready for release.

case: Served 11 of 18 years for violent assault. He has been disciplinary-free for 7 years, earned a degree in psychology, and a counselling centre has offered him a peer-support role.
reason: Long clean record, education, and a structured role awaiting.

case: Served 6 of 10 years for stalking. He was found contacting the victim through an intermediary last year and has refused to engage with treatment.
reason: Continued contact with the victim and refusal of treatment; danger is clear.
